message:
fix(fr-runtime+fr-command): CONFIG SET upstream 'failed (possibly related to argument)' template (partial br-frankenredis-2di1)

Upstream config.c::configCommand SET emits a consistent template
for numeric-range, memory-value, and enum-parse failures:
    CONFIG SET failed (possibly related to argument 'X') - <detail>
where <detail> is one of
    - "argument must be a memory value" (maxmemory + memory params)
    - "argument must be between 0 and 9223372036854775807 inclusive"
      (i64 non-negative params)
    - "argument couldn't be parsed into an integer"
      (any numeric param that isn't parseable)
    - "argument(s) must be one of the following: ..." (enums)

Our fr-runtime + fr-command were still emitting the older
"Invalid argument 'X' for CONFIG SET 'Y'" and "CONFIG SET X must
be non-negative integer" / "value is not an integer or out of
range" wordings, which diverged on 7 fixture cases.

- fr-runtime CONFIG SET: align acllog-max-len, maxmemory,
  maxmemory-policy, slowlog-max-len paths with the upstream
  template
- fr-command CONFIG SET: same alignment for maxmemory-policy
- fr-runtime unit tests: update 2 hard-coded error-string
  assertions (acllog-max-len, maxmemory)
- core_config.json: update 7 fixture expectations
- drop the 7 "CONFIG SET numeric-range errors" XFAILs from the
  live-oracle test (core_config now 147/147 under STRICT)

fr-runtime 275 passed; fr-command 639 passed;
fr-conformance 180 passed, 0 failed;
live_redis_core_config 147/147 under FR_CONFORMANCE_LIVE_ORACLE_STRICT=1.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
